source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Synchronous slash command (no LLM call)
notes: Spec asserts: state stays ready throughout the slash command, no →working transition recorded for the slash command itself. The recipe issues /cost and /help (both genuinely local — see parser.go isLocalCommandContent, which filters their <local-command> wrappers from the transcript). A trailing tiny normal turn is included to prove the session is still healthy and CAN drive ready→working when given a real prompt — this catches a degenerate-pass where the session simply never woke up. The slash_window phase pins the no-→working invariant with duration_at_least_ms 4000 so both slash commands are inside it.

text: Both /cost and /help fire inside this window. State stays ready throughout — no LLM call, no →working transition (spec bullets 1 and 2)
After the slash-command window, a normal user prompt drives ready → working (proves the session is still healthy and slash commands didn't deadlock it)
Normal turn completes back to ready
